what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML preview card for a brand staged-process flow pattern, showing four outlined stage cards connected by dashed gold arrows with supporting description and style metadata.
observations: The file includes a dsCard metadata comment naming the card, group, subtitle, and viewport.
It links to _card.css and defines file-local CSS for the preview layout and process-flow elements.
The flow is horizontal, centered, and built from stage cards plus dashed arrow connector elements.
Stage cards use gold borders, bronze display titles, secondary body text, and optional cream emphasis via the cream class.
A .stage.gap variant is styled for a smaller between-stage pill but is not used in the rendered markup.
Arrowheads are created with a CSS ::after pseudo-element using borders.
The bottom meta row documents border, connector, and title styling choices.
standouts: Dashed gold connector arrows are a central visual device.
Cream-tinted stage cards provide alternating emphasis within the same flow.
The file documents the visual recipe inside the preview itself through lead copy and meta text.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from the shared card stylesheet for typography, colors, radius, and canvas background.
Keeps all preview-specific styling inline in the HTML file.
Uses semantic-light div structure for a visual design-system specimen rather than a reusable component API.
Follows a design-card preview pattern with lead text, specimen content, and compact meta annotations.
concerns: The horizontal flow has fixed minimum stage widths and no wrapping behavior, which may overflow the declared 700px viewport.
The lead copy describes a between-stage gap card, and CSS defines .stage.gap, but the example markup does not render one.
notes: A compact design-system specimen for a branded horizontal process flow using outlined stage cards and dashed gold arrow connectors.
questions: Should the gap-card variant be shown in the example flow or only documented as an available variant?
Is the declared 700px viewport expected to accommodate horizontal overflow, scaling, or clipping?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered staged process flow; the gap-card variant is described and styled but not displayed.
intention_signals: It appears intended to preserve a repeatable brand pattern for showing sequential project stages with premium-looking gold connectors and optional emphasis states.
novelty: The smaller between-stage gap-card idea, styled as a pill on the connector, is distinct within this file even though it is not rendered.
contribution: Contributes a file-local visual substrate for staged process diagrams: gold-outlined stage cards, dashed arrow connectors with CSS arrowheads, cream emphasis states, and compact styling annotations that a fused system could retain as a process-flow pattern.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone HTML design-system preview card showing a horizontal branded staged-process flow with gold outlined cards, dashed gold arrows, cream emphasis states, and meta styling notes.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for documenting and previewing a reusable brand process-flow pattern for staged work or delivery sequences.
